author: lOrm
content:
## OC current-behavior spec (HEAD ba62e46d) ‚Äî Phase-2 pin

Describes what `src/mcp.rs` does TODAY; future refactors must preserve these contracts.

## B1 ‚Äî `McpServer::new` handshake (`initialize` + `tools/list`)
**OC location**: `src/mcp.rs:778-855` (`new` ‚Üí `new_with_config`), `:858-911` (`initialize`).
**Contract**: `(name: &str, transport: Box<dyn McpTransport>) -> Result<McpServer, McpError>`. Default config (`McpServerConfig::new()`) uses a 30 s deadline. The struct starts with `info: None`, `capabilities: McpCapabilities::default()`, `tools: Vec::new()`, then drives `initialize()` and `refresh_tools()` either (a) raw when `initialize_timeout_secs == 0`, or (b) each wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout(deadline, ‚Ä¶)`. `initialize()` sends `protocolVersion: "2024-11-05"`, declares `capabilities.roots.listChanged: true` (no `elicitation`, no `sampling`), `clientInfo.name = "openclaudia"`, `clientInfo.version = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")`. `serverInfo` and `capabilities` parse via `.ok()` / `.unwrap_or_default()` ‚Äî malformed metadata silently falls back to defaults. A `notifications/initialized` request follows; its `Result` is discarded with `.ok()`.
**Edge cases**: Timeout `0` disables the deadline. On timeout the inner future is cancelled (drops in-flight `read_until`); the child process is NOT killed. `notifications/initialized` returning `Err` is intentionally swallowed.
**Error paths**: `McpError::Timeout { phase: "initialize" }` and `McpError::Timeout { phase: "tools/list" }` on deadline expiry; otherwise transport's `McpError` propagates.
**Invariants**: `protocolVersion` is hard-coded ‚Äî never negotiated. `clientInfo` is identical across all servers.

## B2 ‚Äî `refresh_tools` capability-gated discovery
**OC location**: `src/mcp.rs:938-974`; gate via `has_tools_capability()` `:920-923`.
**Contract**: `&mut self -> Result<(), McpError>`. If `!self.has_tools_capability()` (i.e. `capabilities.tools.is_none()`), log `debug!` and return `Ok(())` with no wire call. `self.tools` is LEFT UNTOUCHED ‚Äî a previous successful refresh survives. Otherwise sends `tools/list` with `None` params. `result.get("tools").and_then(as_array)` is `serde_json::from_value::<McpTool>(‚Ä¶).ok()` and `filter_map`-collected ‚Äî malformed entries are silently dropped. A single `info!` logs `tool_count` and `list_changed_supported` derived from `capabilities.tools.list_changed`.
**Edge cases**: Result with no `tools` key or non-array value ‚Üí `self.tools` unchanged (no clear). Tool deserialization is best-effort.
**Error paths**: `self.transport.request("tools/list", None).await?` propagates verbatim; no retry/fallback.

## B3 ‚Äî `call_tool` with `isError` envelope inspection
**OC location**: `src/mcp.rs:1007-1057`.
**Contract**: `(name: &str, arguments: Value) -> Result<Value, McpError>`. Pre-flight: `self.tools.iter().any(|t| t.name == name)` ‚Äî on miss returns `McpError::ToolNotFound(name)` without making a wire call. Otherwise sends `tools/call` with `params = json!({"name": name, "arguments": arguments})`. Per fix #625, inspects `result.get("isError").and_then(as_bool).unwrap_or(false)`. If true, extracts `content[*].text` via `arr.iter().find_map(...)` and returns `McpError::ToolReportedError { message }`. If `content` is missing or contains no text, message defaults to `"MCP tool '{name}' returned isError with no content"`. Otherwise returns the raw result `Value`.
**Edge cases**: `isError: false`, absent, or non-boolean ‚Üí treated as success. Pre-flight uses the locally cached `tools` list ‚Äî a tool registered after the last `refresh_tools` is not callable.
**Error paths**: `ToolNotFound`, `ToolReportedError`, or transport variant.

## B4 ‚Äî `list_resources` / `has_resources` capability gate
**OC location**: `src/mcp.rs:1061-1097`. `has_resources()` is a `const fn` checking `capabilities.resources.is_some()`.
**Contract**: If `!has_resources()`, returns `Ok(Vec::new())` with no wire call. Otherwise sends `resources/list` with `Some(json!({}))` as params (NOT `None`). The `resources` array is `filter_map`-deserialized into `Vec<McpResource>`; missing array ‚Üí `Vec::new()`.
**Edge cases**: Empty `{}` is sent on the wire ‚Äî not `null`. Resource entries that fail to deserialize are silently dropped.
**Error paths**: Transport errors propagate; missing `resources` field yields `Ok(vec![])`.

## B5 ‚Äî `read_resource` text-first then blob fallback
**OC location**: `src/mcp.rs:1104-1135`.
**Contract**: Sends `resources/read` with `params = json!({"uri": uri})`. Walks `result.contents`: (a) collects all `text` fields and joins with `\n`; (b) if none, collects all `blob` fields and joins with `\n`; (c) if both empty, returns `result.to_string()` (full JSON-RPC result serialized).
**Edge cases**: Mixed text+blob ‚Üí only text items are returned. No capability gate ‚Äî call is unconditional even when the server did not advertise `resources` capability (asymmetric with `list_resources`).
**Error paths**: Transport error propagated.

## B6 ‚Äî Transport JSON-RPC error surface
**OC location**: `StdioTransport::request` `src/mcp.rs:374-540`, `HttpTransport::request` separately.
**Contract**: JSON-RPC error responses format as `McpError::Protocol("RPC error {code}: {message}{data_info}")`; stdio appends `" data: {data}"` when `error.data` is present, HTTP variant drops `data`. ID mismatch yields a dedicated variant (fix #701). Oversized stdio response (`> MAX_RESPONSE_SIZE` without newline) returns `McpError::Transport(...)` with an stderr snippet appended. EOF before first byte returns `McpError::Transport("MCP server closed stdout before responding{snippet}")`.
**Edge cases**: Stdio uses a single `Mutex<BufReader>` plus a `request_lock` (fix #732) ‚Äî the write+read transaction is serialized so the server sees one in-flight request at a time. Oversized line is detected BEFORE allocation via `Take::read_until` (fix #445). Request IDs are monotonic `AtomicU64::fetch_add(1, SeqCst)` starting at 1 ‚Äî id 0 is never sent.
**Error paths**: `McpError::Transport(String)` for IO + size violations; `McpError::Protocol(String)` for parse + RPC errors; `McpError::Timeout { phase }` for handshake only (steady-state stdio has no built-in timeout; HTTP has `HTTP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT`).
**Invariants**: No reconnection / cache-clear after disconnect ‚Äî a broken transport remains in `McpManager`'s map until rebuilt via `ConnectionSpec::build_transport()` (fix #629).
